questions.question:
Consider two hosts \( \text{X} \) and \( \text{Y} \), connected by a single direct link of rate \( 10^6\; \text{bits/sec} \). The distance between the two hosts is \( 10,000\;\text{km} \) and the propagation speed along the link is \( 2 \times 10^8 \;\text{m/sec} \). Host \( \text{X} \) send a file of \( 50,000\;\text{bytes} \) as one large message to host \( \text{Y} \) continuously. Let the transmission and propagation delays be \( \text{p milliseconds} \) and \( \text{q milliseconds}, \) respectively. Then the value of \( \text{p} \) and \( \text{q} \) are



Computer Networks: NIELIT 2022 April Scientist B | Section B | Question: 101
